title: Krudar

{{Quote|Why don't you watch where you're going, human?|Krudar|<ref name="W">{{DarkStryder|adventure=Wildfire (adventure)|book=The Kathol Outback}}</ref>}}
'''Krudar''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Duros/Legends|Duros]] [[Soldier/Legends|soldier]] and [[Gunner/Legends|gunner]] who served with the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]], [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]], and the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] during his career. Originally a member of the Rebel Alliance, Krudar left their service after becoming convinced that the organization used their mandate of bringing freedom to [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] to justify acts of [[Terrorist/Legends|terrorism]] and violence. Joining a [[Unidentified pirate group (Galactic Civil War)|group of pirates]], Krudar befriended the [[Guudrian]] [[Boom]], and crewed with them until the gang was destroyed by [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] forces.

Hiding out on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Kal'Shebbol/Legends|Kal'Shebbol]] in the [[Kathol sector/Legends|Kathol sector]] with Boom, Krudar was present when the New Republic launched an [[Battle of Kal'Shebbol|assault]] on the world to liberate it from the rule of Imperial [[Warlord/Legends|warlord]] [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Kentor Sarne]]. Despite the successful freeing of the planet, Moff Sarne managed to escape into the Kathol [[Sector/Legends|sector]] with the majority of his [[Moff Sarne's forces|forces]]. The New Republic authorized the [[CR90 corvette/Legends|CR90 corvette]] ''[[FarStar]]'' to track down the rogue warlord and neutralize any threat he posed to the region, but was forced to recruit from the civilian population of Kal'Shebbol to crew the [[Starship/Legends|vessel]] due to manpower shortages. Krudar joined the [[DarkStryder Campaign|mission]] and convinced the New Republic selectors to accept Boom as well. The Duros served on the ''FarStar'' throughout its mission into the Kathol sector, participating in several operations along the way. However, his surly and temperamental attitude did little to endear him to the rest of the crew, and he was involved in several confrontations with other personnel during the mission. Several [[Standard month/Legends|months]] after leaving Kal'Shebbol, Krudar participated in the [[Battle of Kathol System]], an [[Space warfare/Legends|engagement]] that saw the end of Moff Sarne.

==Biography==
===Early career===
[[File:MedicalFrigate-BaseSeries4.png|thumb|right|An EF76 Nebulon-B escort frigate, similar to the vessel Krudar served on.]]
Krudar, a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Duros/Legends|Duros]],<ref name="TDC" /> served as a member of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. Shortly after the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], Krudar was an enlisted [[Soldier/Legends|soldier]] stationed at the Alliance's [[Titan Base]] in the [[Corva sector/Legends|Corva sector]]. He was present in Titan Base's barracks cleaning his weapon when a group of [[operative]]s arrived who were tasked with a [[Operation: Shell Game|mission]] to infiltrate the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] base known as [[HM7-R|"The Hammer"]].<ref name="OSG">{{FFG|story=Operation: Shell Game (adventure)|book=Star Wars: Age of Rebellion Beta|text=''Star Wars: Age of Rebellion'' Beta}}</ref> Krudar later served as a [[Turbolaser/Legends|turbolaser]] [[Gunner/Legends|gunner]] aboard an Alliance [[EF76 Nebulon-B escort frigate/Legends|EF76 Nebulon-B escort frigate]].<ref name="TDC" />

However, Krudar became disenchanted with the Rebellion's motives, believing that their claim to be fighting for justice and freedom was actually a warped justification for them to commit acts of war and [[Terrorist/Legends|terrorism]]. In addition, Krudar had yet to receive any tangible benefits since joining the Rebellion, and jumped [[Starship/Legends|ship]] to join a [[Unidentified pirate group (Galactic Civil War)|pirate band]]. The [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]] that Krudar worked with were not particularly fussy about the targets they engaged, and the Duros found himself manning his weapon against Rebel, Imperial, and neutral vessels. During a boarding action on an Imperial vessel, Krudar obtained a [[Blast vest/Legends|blast vest]] and [[Blast helmet/Legends|helmet]] from a dead [[Imperial gunner/Legends|Imperial gunner]]. Taking them for his own, he wore them as part of his uniform, and decorated the blast vest with small red metal studs. Each stud represented one of Krudar's starship or [[Capital ship/Legends|capital ship]] kills. During his time with the pirates, he met the [[Guudrian]] [[Boom]], and the two developed a friendship.<ref name="TDC" />

Eventually, the Empire took notice of the pirates, and the gang was destroyed by Imperial forces. Krudar managed to escape the destruction of the group, and fled to the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]] with Boom. For a time, the pair served as gunners on various [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggling]] [[Freighter/Legends|freighters]] before arriving on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Kal'Shebbol/Legends|Kal'Shebbol]]—shortly before its [[Battle of Kal'Shebbol|liberation]] by the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]]—to lie low.<ref name="TDC" />

===Joining the ''FarStar''===
[[File:FarStar.jpg|thumb|right|The CR90 corvette ''FarStar'']]
When the New Republic liberated Kal'Shebbol, the planet's ruler, [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Kentor Sarne]], escaped into the [[Kathol sector/Legends|Kathol sector]] with the bulk of his [[Moff Sarne's forces|military forces]]; because of this, he was still considered to be a threat to the security of the region.<ref name="TSB">{{DarkStryder|adventure=The Saga Begins|book=The DarkStryder Campaign}}</ref> Learning that the New Republic had commissioned the [[CR90 corvette/Legends|CR90 corvette]] ''[[FarStar]]'' to track down the rogue [[Warlord/Legends|warlord]], Krudar signed onto the ship as a gunner, hoping to add a few more kill markers to his blast vest. The Duros also managed to convince the selectors to take on Boom, telling them that his expertise with explosives would be an asset on the [[DarkStryder Campaign|mission]]. Krudar kept his past a secret from his new crewmates, and Boom would not divulge anything about it either. He spent much of his time on the ship with the turbolasers, either checking them out or standing duty by them.<ref name="TDC" />

Several [[Standard week/Legends|weeks]] into the mission, Krudar participated in a gunnery contest shortly after leaving the planet [[Binaros]], in an attempt to win an extended shore leave at the ''FarStar''{{'s}} next stopover. During the contest, his turbolaser began sparking and malfunctioned, and the Duros believed that one of the other crewmembers had sabotaged his weapon in order to stop him from winning the contest. The malfunction had in fact been caused by the [[feeder plant]], which the ''FarStar'' had inadvertently picked up during their mission to Binaros. The mobile plants had extracted certain nutrients and compounds from areas of the ship, and had caused damage to several systems during their explorations. The ship's [[Xenobiologist/Legends|xenobiologist]], [[Varel DeVay]], managed to concoct a gaseous [[Poison/Legends|poison]] to kill the plant before it became a true danger to the ship.<ref name="PF">{{DarkStryder|adventure=Plant Food|book=The Kathol Outback}}</ref>

===Rising tensions===
Several weeks later, Krudar was in the [[Bridge (starship)/Legends|command center]] when he was involved in an altercation with [[Ensign/Legends|Ensign]] [[Colton (FarStar crewmember)|Colton]], one of the few regular New Republic [[New Republic Defense Fleet/Legends|naval]] crewmen assigned to the vessel. Both were suffering from the effects of a [[Qektoth Confederation biochemical agent|biochemical agent]] developed by the [[Qektoth Confederation]], a group of rogue [[Scientist/Legends|scientists]] who believed in the superiority of biological technology. The crew of the [[Corvette/Legends|corvette]] had been exposed to the agent after they had rescued two Imperial crewmen from a damaged [[Unidentified Gamma-class assault shuttle|''Gamma''-class assault shuttle]] that the Confederation had used as a testbed for their new weapon. The agent caused Krudar to be more antagonistic and belligerent than usual, and he had to be separated from Colton before the argument could escalate into a proper fight. Both Krudar and Colton were temporarily confined to quarters by [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Kaiya Adrimetrum]] despite their protests.<ref name="W" />

[[File:Guudrian.jpg|thumb|left|Boom, Krudar's Guudrian friend]]
While traveling through the volatile area of space known as the [[Kathol Rift/Legends|Kathol Rift]], the crew of the ''FarStar'' began to suffer from various psychological effects, ranging from paranoid feelings to full-blown hallucinations. The effects of the Rift were well-documented, and many crews passing through the area had reported similar experiences. Krudar's mild [[agoraphobia]] was intensified by the Rift's effects, and the feelings he experienced sometimes kept him from leaving his quarters. If he attempted to exit his quarters to attend to his duty station, he would perceive the corridors of the ship lengthening into the distance. The feeling would only diminish when the gunner returned to his quarters.<ref name="KR">''[[The Kathol Rift]]''</ref>

The constant pressure involved in crewing the ''FarStar'' began to irritate Krudar, leading him to be more aggressive, surly, and impatient. After he was involved in several scuffles with various crew members, the command crew warned Krudar that if he participated in any more altercations, he would be stripped of his rank and position as well as confined to his quarters for the rest of the mission. To add to his worries, his friend Boom had developed into a major source of agitation. The Guudrian's love of explosions and pyrotechnics had led him to experiment with several of the crew's personal possessions by setting them on fire to see how they interacted with heat and flames. Although Krudar attempted to restrain Boom, he had a hard job of it, and the situation did little to change the command crew's opinion of the Duros, as he had vouched for Boom to join the crew in the first place. As a result, Krudar became frustrated with Boom as his actions made it harder for the gunner to keep a low profile with the crew.<ref name="KR" />

===The Battle of Kathol system===
[[File:Battleofkathol.jpg|thumb|right|The Battle of the Kathol system]]
The ''FarStar'' eventually reached the [[Kathol system/Legends|Kathol system]] after several [[Standard month/Legends|months]], which was the location of Moff Sarne's new power base and home to the mysterious [[DarkStryder]], an [[Insectoid/Legends|insectoid]] entity that was responsible for the creation of the exotic [[DarkStryder technology]] that Sarne coveted. Krudar stood to his assigned duty, and participated in the [[Battle of Kathol System]], an engagement that would see the end of Moff Sarne, the destruction of the majority of his forces, the [[Death/Legends|death]] of the mysterious DarkStryder, and the loss of the ''FarStar'' itself.<ref name="E">''[[Endgame]]''</ref>

==Personality and traits==
A [[Color/Legends|green]]-skinned, red-eyed Duros, Krudar projected the façade of being a stereotypical member of his species, appearing to be calm and peaceful. In reality, however, he enjoyed the thrill of [[Space warfare/Legends|ship-to-ship combat]] and the prestige that came with it. His main concerns were material gain and keeping track of his kills.<ref name="TDC" /> During his time with the Rebellion, Krudar was amenable to newcomers to Titan Base, but still regarded them indifferently until they had proven themselves as being reliable and competent.<ref name="OSG" /> He only spoke when he had to,<ref name="TDC" /> unlike the majority of his species who often enjoyed company and telling stories of their adventures.<ref name="GG4">[[Galaxy Guide 4: Alien Races (Second Edition)|''Galaxy Guide 4: Alien Races'', Second Edition]]</ref> However, he did translate for his companion, Boom, when it was necessary. Krudar was also very protective of the weapons under his care, and strove to do the best he could to keep them in the best condition possible.<ref name="TDC" />

[[File:Krudarguns.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Krudar manning one of the ''FarStar''{{'s}} turbolasers]]
During his time on the ''FarStar'', the relentless pressure involved in running the vessel at peak operating condition with no support network began to take its toll on Krudar, and his carefully controlled belligerency came to the surface more often. This resulted in arguments and confrontations with various crewmembers,<ref name="KR" /> and he sometimes believed that his shipmates were deliberately trying to antagonize him by rigging his weapon to malfunction,<ref name="PF" /> or attempting to draw him into an altercation when they knew he was being watched for any signs of hostility.<ref name="KR" /> Spending weeks cooped up on the ''FarStar'' made Krudar more surly and irritable than usual and, while infected with a biochemical agent devised by the Qektoth Confederation, the Duros was unable to control his feelings, which led to a public confrontation with Ensign Colton on the bridge of the ''FarStar''.<ref name="W" />

Krudar maintained a friendship with Boom from his days as a pirate, which extended to the Duros convincing the New Republic to allow the Guudrian aboard the ''FarStar''.<ref name="TDC" /> However, Boom's overriding interest in explosions and melting objects did not endear him to the crew of the vessel, and Krudar suffered from the crew's impatience and frustration with his friend. As the only one who could translate for Boom, Krudar often found himself forced to try and control his friend's behavior, something that became harder and harder as the mission progressed.<ref name="KR" />

Mildly agoraphobic, Krudar preferred to spend free time in his quarters rather than socializing with his shipmates. While his condition was not a full-blown phobia and did not affect his duties, he found his fears were intensified by the effects of the Kathol Rift during the ''FarStar''{{'s}} journey through the region of space that it occupied.<ref name="KR" />

==Skills and abilities==
Krudar was trained in a variety of gunnery disciplines, including blaster [[Artillery/Legends|artillery]], vehicle-mounted blasters, [[Capital ship/Legends|capital ship]] and starship-mounted weapons. He was also capable of repairing weapons on starships and capital ships, and was knowledgeable of other [[Alien/Legends|alien]] [[Species/Legends|species]].<ref name="TDC" />

==Equipment==
Krudar generally armed himself with a [[Blaster pistol/Legends|blaster pistol]] and carried a [[repair kit]] while on duty. He wore a black blast helmet adorned with a personalized sigil, and a grey and red blast vest decorated with small metal studs.<ref name="TDC" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:TheDarkStryderCampaign.jpg|thumb|right|The cover of ''The DarkStryder Campaign''.]]
Krudar was created for [[West End Games]]' ''[[The DarkStryder Campaign]]'',<ref name="TDC" /> published on [[July 17]], [[1995]].<ref name="BN">{{BN|url=w/star-wars-the-roleplaying-game-box-set/1018175954|text=Star Wars the Roleplaying Game: Darkstryder Campaign Boxed Set}}</ref> A member of the support crew of the ''FarStar'', Krudar was referenced in all three of the subsequent supplements to the [[The DarkStryder Campaign (series)|campaign]].<ref name="KO">''[[The Kathol Outback]]''</ref><ref name="KR" /><ref name="E" /> ''The DarkStryder Campaign'' suggested that Krudar be kept alive during the events of the campaign, but that the gamemaster was free to develop his personality as necessary.<ref name="TDC" /> In the "Crew Development" section of ''[[Endgame]]'', it is suggested that he will likely go down with the ''FarStar'' during the space battle, although it is noted that he may come in handy during the ground battle as well.<ref name="E" />
